title: Dashboard
subtitle: A more trustworthy workspace for data sync, strategy execution, and portfolio state.

empty.positions: No positions yet. Open a paper position or deploy a strategy so this table starts collecting real data.
empty.orders: No recent orders. Paper or live execution activity will show up here first.
empty.alerts: No alerts have fired yet. Create a price reminder or risk alert to start monitoring.
empty.navExplanation: A NAV curve appears after you complete at least one backtest or start tracking the first position over time.
empty.allocationTitle: Allocation appears after the first position
empty.allocationExplanation: Once the portfolio contains live or simulated holdings, this panel will explain how capital is distributed.

health.warningDescription: There are still {{count}} missing trading days, so analytics may be incomplete.
health.okDescription: Recent sync activity looks normal, so strategy and portfolio analytics should be easier to trust.

nextActions.heading: Next best actions
nextActions.subheading: These are the fastest steps to turn an empty workspace into something observable and trustworthy.

sync.title: Sync your first data batch
sync.description: Start by checking data coverage so signals, analytics, and system health all rest on something explainable.

strategy.title: Create your first strategy
strategy.description: Start with a template or a simple rule so QuantMate can produce the first piece of research output.

backtest.title: Run the first backtest
backtest.description: One completed backtest is enough to unlock much more useful return, drawdown, and comparison views.

paper.title: Start paper trading
paper.description: Move the strongest idea into a simulated account to validate execution, exposure, and monitoring rules.

setup.title: Wake QuantMate up with the first real workflow
setup.explanation: The dashboard is still waiting on data, a starter strategy, and the first backtest result. Once those arrive, the KPI cards and charts become much more informative.
setup.helper: A solid first milestone is: sync data, clone a template, run a backtest, then move the best idea into paper trading.

activity.heading: Recent activity
activity.subheading: Orders and alerts appear here first, so you can tell whether the workspace is moving or still waiting on setup.
activity.emptyExplanation: As soon as orders execute or alerts trigger, this timeline becomes the fastest operational summary on the page.
